message:
feat(engine): add module_async_evaluation for ES2020 async module evaluation ordering

Introduce the module_async_evaluation module implementing ES2020 §15.2.1.16
async module evaluation semantics:

- Modules with top-level `await` produce a Promise for their evaluation
- Modules importing from an async module are themselves async
- Rejection in an async module propagates through the module graph
- Live bindings from rejected modules enter the `Dead` state

The module builds on the existing esm_loader (ModuleStatus, EsmModule),
module_live_binding (LiveBindingMap, BindingCell), promise_model
(PromiseHandle, PromiseState, PromiseStore), and object_model (JsValue)
foundations. It tracks AsyncModulePhase (Synchronous, Suspended, Settled,
Rejected) per module, manages suspension/settlement ordering, and propagates
rejection-triggered binding death through dependent modules.

Registered as `pub mod module_async_evaluation` in lib.rs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
